ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: Implementation of algorithms for cutting numerical values exhibiting a potentially highly skewed distribution into evenly distributed groups (bins). This functionality can be applied for binning discrete values, such as counts, as well as for discretization of continuous values, for example, during generation of features used in machine learning algorithms.

Implementation of algorithms for cutting numerical values exhibiting a potentially highly skewed distribution into evenly distributed groups (bins). This functionality can be applied for binning discrete values, such as counts, as well as for discretization of continuous values, for example, during generation of features used in machine learning algorithms.

name: r-binr
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = "1.1.2" %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-binr
  version: {{ version|replace("-", "_") }}

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/binr_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/binr/binr_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 67d08164f0da849dd9b5d7402e8d5b84273377716f2de31dd3882b06f210626f

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
  host:
    - r-base
  run:
    - r-base

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('binr')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('binr')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: http://github.com/jabiru/binr
  license: Apache-2.0
  summary: Implementation of algorithms for cutting numerical values exhibiting a potentially highly skewed distribution into evenly distributed groups (bins). This functionality can be applied for binning discrete values, such as counts, as well as for discretization of continuous values, for example, during 
    generation of features used in machine learning algorithms.
  license_family: APACHE
  license_file: LICENSE

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r

# Package: binr
# Title: Cut Numeric Values into Evenly Distributed Groups
# Version: 1.1
# Author: Sergei Izrailev
# Maintainer: Sergei Izrailev <sizrailev@jabiruventures.com>
# Description: Implementation of algorithms for cutting numerical values exhibiting a potentially highly skewed distribution into evenly distributed groups (bins). This functionality can be applied for binning discrete values, such as counts, as well as for discretization of continuous values, for example, during generation of features used in machine learning algorithms.
# URL: http://github.com/jabiru/binr
# Depends: R (>= 2.15),
# License: Apache License (== 2.0)
# Copyright: Copyright (C) Collective, Inc. | file inst/COPYRIGHTS
# LazyData: true
# Packaged: 2015-03-10 18:32:18 UTC; sergei
# NeedsCompilation: no
# Repository: CRAN
